bids.reasoning: Since this is the opening offer at the very beginning of the negotiation, I will propose a bid that maximizes my utility to establish a strong anchor. I am targeting my most preferred values across all issues, particularly on my highest-weighted issues F, E, and B, while I leave room for future concessions on my lower-weighted issues.

It is very early in the negotiation (t=0.02). The opponent's first offer matches my top preference for issueE (valueA). To show a willingness to compromise without giving up significant utility this early, I will concede completely on issueD (which has zero weight for me) by offering the opponent's preferred valueA, while maintaining my top preferences for all other issues.

We are in the early stages of the negotiation (t=0.04), so I will keep my utility very high. The opponent has requested `valueA` for `issueE`, which perfectly aligns with my highest utility for that issue, so I will lock that in. I noticed the opponent originally asked for `valueA` on `issueA`, which still gives me a reasonably high utility (0.66), so I will concede on that issue while keeping the highest utility values on my most important ones (`issueF`, `issueB`, and `issueC`).

The negotiation is still very early (t=0.06). The opponent has offered mutually beneficial values on issueE (valueA) and issueD (valueB). I will accept these values to build momentum and hold firm on my most critical issues (issueF and issueB), maintaining a high utility target. I will also incorporate their valueA for issueA as a continuing concession.

The negotiation is still in the early stages (t=0.08). The opponent has been consistently prioritizing their preferences on my highest weighted issues (issueB and issueF), though we align perfectly on issueE (valueA) and closely on issueD (valueB). To maintain a relatively high utility for myself while demonstrating flexibility, I will hold firm on issueB, issueE, and issueF, while offering a slight concession on issueC (moving to valueB) and maintaining my previous concession on issueA (valueA) paired with the opponent's preferred value for issueD.

The negotiation is still early at t=0.1, but I observe the opponent strongly prefers issueA=valueA, issueC=valueA, and issueD=valueB. I am holding firm on my highest-weighted issues (issueF=valueC, issueB=valueC) and my preferred issueE=valueA (which they have also previously offered). I will fully concede to their preferred value on issueC (valueA) to demonstrate flexibility and invite alignment.

We are still very early in the negotiation process (t=0.12), so my strategy is to maintain a high utility target while sending consistent signals on my critical issues. We have quickly found common ground on issues E and D, which both parties seem to prefer as valueA and valueB respectively. I will hold firm on my most important issues (F and B) while floating a slight variation on issues A and C to explore overlapping preferences.

The negotiation is still in its early stages (t=0.14). My objective is to explore the opponent's flexibility while maintaining high utility for my most important issues (issueF, issueE, and issueB). Since the opponent has frequently requested valueA for issueA and issueD, I will concede on these lower-weight topics, and soften my stance to valueB on issueC to offer a reasonably fresh combination while aiming for a utility of around 0.81.

At time t=0.16, I am still in the early phase of the negotiation, so I will maintain a relatively high utility for myself while attempting to compromise. I will hold firm on high-importance issues (issueB, issueE, issueF) by offering my preferred values, as they carry the majority of my weight. To move towards an agreement, I am conceding on the lower-importance issues (issueC and issueD) by proposing the values the opponent has repeatedly requested in their past offers.

The opponent significantly conceded on my most important issue (issueF: valueC) and also aligned with issueD: valueB in their last offer. I will retain these values and offer another concession by matching their constant demand for issueC: valueA. I am keeping my high-priority issues (issueB and issueE) at my preferred values to maintain a utility of 0.82 since it is still early in the negotiation.

The current time fraction is 0.20, leaving plenty of room for negotiation. The opponent has previously shown a willingness to accept `issueE: valueA` and recently offered `issueF: valueC`, both of which align perfectly with my highest utility preferences. Thus, I will hold firm on these two issues alongside `issueB`, while offering a mild concession on `issueC` (moving to `valueB`) and adopting their preferred value for `issueD` (where I have zero weight). This helps us find middle ground without dropping my overall utility too sharply early on.

The negotiation is still in the early stages (t=0.22). The opponent recently shifted to offer `valueC` for `issueC` and `valueA` for `issueE`, both of which are my highest utility preferences for those issues. I will lock in these agreements alongside my top preferences for my high-weight issues (`issueF`, `issueB`). To demonstrate cooperative compromise, I will concede to the opponent's strong preference for `valueA` on `issueA`, reducing my utility slightly but maintaining a strong overall value well above 0.85.

I am prioritizing my highest-weighted issues (F, E, and B), while looking for mutually acceptable values on the others based on the opponent's history. The opponent frequently requests issueE: valueA, which aligns perfectly with my highest utility. In their latest offer, the opponent requested issueD: valueC; since issueD has zero importance to me, I will concede on it to demonstrate flexibility without sacrificing my overall utility, maintaining a strong offer above 0.85 utility.

The opponent has shown willingness to concede on `issueB` and `issueE` by offering `valueC` and `valueA`, respectively, matching my max utilities. I will hold firm on these issues and my highest-weighted issue (`issueF`), maintaining a high overall utility while offering concessions on `issueC` and `issueD` to values they have proposed in the past. At this early-mid stage (t=0.26), I maintain a target utility safely above 0.80.

The time fraction is 0.28, which means we are approaching the mid-game. My strategy is to hold firm on my highest-priority issues (issueF, issueE, and issueB) by demanding valueC, valueA, and valueC respectively, securing the majority of my utility. I am willing to partially concede on lower-weight issues like issueD and issueC by matching the opponent's recent offer of valueB for both, demonstrating flexibility without sacrificing significant utility.

At t=0.30, I am maintaining high utility on my three most important issues (F, E, and B), from which I derive 0.70 total utility. I am offering concessions on the remaining issues (A, C, and D) by suggesting 'valueA' for issueA and 'valueC' for issueD, reflecting the opponent's repeated inclusion of these values in their recent offers while maintaining my utility above 0.80.

We are still relatively early in the negotiation (t=0.32), so I will maintain a fairly high target utility around 0.82 while actively exploring combinations that yield mutual gains. I noticed the opponent heavily favors `valueB` for `issueA`, so I will offer that as a concession on my lowest-weighted positive issue. In exchange, I will hold firm on `issueE` and `issueF` (which the opponent recently matched) and my top values for `issueB` and `issueC`, ensuring my core interests are protected.

I am targeting high utility (~0.82) since we are still in the early-mid stages (t=0.34). The opponent just offered `issueA: valueC` and `issueD: valueB`, both of which align perfectly with my maximum utility for those specific issues. I will incorporate these into my offer while retaining my top preferences for my high-weight issues (B, E, F), and conceding on issue C (`valueA`) which the opponent has frequently bid.

I am prioritizing my highest-weighted issues (has issueF, issueE, and issueB maintaining max utility) to ensure I retain significant value. To move closer to an agreement at this moderate stage in the negotiation, I am testing variations in the lower-weighted issues (issueA, issueC, and issueD), specifically incorporating valueC for issueD and valueB for issueC, which have appeared in the opponent's offers. This allows me to concede efficiently without sacrificing my primary objectives.

The opponent's most recent offer included `valueC` for `issueA`, `valueA` for `issueC`, and `valueA` for `issueD`. Since `issueD` is of no importance to me and accommodating the opponent's preferences on `issueA` and `issueC` still provides me with solid utility, I will adopt these values in my current bid to encourage an agreement. I am maintaining firm on my highest-priority issues (`issueB`, `issueE`, `issueF`) to keep my overall utility at a strong 0.82, which is appropriate for the early-mid stage (t=0.38) of this negotiation.

At time 0.4, I am keeping my strategy of defending the most important issues (F, E, and B), maintaining their values at my highest utility (valueC, valueA, and valueC). To make the offer more appealing, I am looking at the opponent's recent bids for my lower-weight issues (A, C, and D). I will match their recent proposals of valueC for issueD, valueA for issueC, and valueC for issueA, yielding an offer with a strong baseline utility of ~0.82 for me while adapting to their demonstrated preferences.

The current time is at t=0.42, which leaves ample time before the deadline. The opponent's most recent offer matched my top preference on `issueF` and `issueC`, and offered a highly acceptable value on `issueA` and `issueD`. I am repeating an earlier strong offer that aligns with these shared preferences (`issueA`: valueA, `issueC`: valueC, `issueD`: valueB, `issueF`: valueC), while firmly holding my ground on `issueB` and `issueE` to maintain a high utility.

We are approaching the midpoint of the negotiation (t=0.44), and the opponent has begun accepting "valueC" on my highest-priority issue (issueF), as well as on issueA. I will secure these gains and maintain my demands on my heavily weighted issues (B, E) while varying issueD to "valueC" (which the opponent has shown some preference for) to explore acceptable combinations at zero cost to my utility.

We are approaching the midpoint of the negotiation (t=0.46). To signal flexibility without significantly harming my own utility, I will compromise on my lower-weight issues (A, C, and D) by offering values the opponent has frequently proposed (e.g., valueA for A, C, and D). I will hold firm on my highest-weight issues (F, E, and B), maintaining them at their maximum utility for me, to secure a total utility of around 0.78.
